TopModule.demo_behavior:
Module: TopModule I would like you to implement a module named TopModule with the following interface. All input and output ports are one bit unless otherwise specified. - input  in  (4 bits) - output pos (2 bits) The module should implement a priority encoder. A priority encoder is a combinational circuit that, when given an input bit vector, outputs the position of the first 1 bit in the vector. For example, a 8-bit priority encoder given the input 8'b10010000 would output 3'd4, because bit[4] is first bit that is high. Build a 4-bit priority encoder. For this problem, if none of the input bits are high (i.e., input is zero), output zero. Note that a 4-bit number has 16 possible combinations.
Key rules: Implement behavior exactly as described in the prompt.
Reset: Only as explicitly described in the prompt.
Corner cases: No additional corner cases beyond prompt requirements.
Performance: Use prompt-defined timing/latency behavior.

test_goals: Pass benchmark harness checks for all provided tests.
verification.oracle_strategy: Use benchmark-provided reference outputs as oracle.
pass_fail_criteria: DUT outputs match oracle outputs for benchmark tests.
